economic_notes: Swan River First Nation holds significant Agricultural Benefits Trust Fund (~$33.3M) managed through RBC Wealth Management including a Minors Trust. Four controlled entities: Swan River Tribal Enterprises Ltd., Wapsewsepi Enterprises Ltd. (revenues $3.6M, net loss $179K), Creeland Resources Inc. (revenues $41K, net loss $19K), and 18805424 Alberta Ltd. First Nation Development Fund active. Housing loan from Bank of Montreal secured by ministerial guarantee.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020. The statement of financial activities shows two different surplus figures: the income statement shows -$249,713 (before other expenditures of amortization -$789,616) yielding -$249,713 net, but the statement of change in net financial assets shows -$429,409. The -$429,409 figure reflects the full consolidated surplus including equity-method entities. Prior year figures similarly differ between statements ($4,174,614 vs $4,262,826). Four entities (Swan River Tribal Enterprises Ltd., Wapsewsepi Enterprises Ltd., Creeland Resources Inc., 18805424 Alberta Ltd.) are accounted for using modified equity method (not fully consolidated). No schedule of remuneration included. Wages and employee benefits per Schedule 4: $3,113,674. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $8,178,281 + Health Canada $2,688,940 + HRDC $375,180 + CMHC $94,753 + LSLIRC $30,000 + FNDF $475,160 = $11,842,314 but FNDF may be provincial/federal lottery fund; conservative total used. expenses_education includes School ($2,182,826) and Post-Secondary ($624,824). Agricultural Benefits Trust distributions treated as pass-through. Large trust fund assets ($33.9M) held by third parties significantly inflate balance sheet.
